title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 Contract · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for node 24, typescript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 contract and requires the smallest defensible typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Choose one amqplib promise or callback API and account for RPC methods, event emitters, callback-only confirms, and non-RPC methods.
Treat delivered bytes, fields, properties, headers, and decoded payload as untrusted runtime data despite TypeScript declarations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Runtime Client Version · changed case · Quiz Runtime Client Version
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 6786 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.

Which action demonstrates Runtime Client Version without dropping earlier contracts?
